title: Segfault in `StringIO` after invalid attempt at reinitialization
author: devdanzin
body:
It's possible to segfault PyPy by calling `__init__` with an invalid argument on an existing `StringIO` instance and ignoring the exception raised, then calling `__next__`:

```python
from io import StringIO

obj = StringIO("1")
try:
    obj.__init__("", ())
except:
    pass
obj.__next__()
```

`pypy -VV`
```
Python 3.10.14 (a05a1649d335, Nov 29 2024, 04:33:11)
[PyPy 7.3.18-alpha0 with GCC 10.2.1 20210130 (Red Hat 10.2.1-11)]
```

Found using [fusil](https://github.com/devdanzin/fusil) by @vstinner.

comments.author: mattip
comments.body:
This is a bit of a stretch. Why would someone expect things to work correctly when ignoring an error?
The fix was simple, so I did it in 53634b2341.
